title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

The Core Information Standard version 2.0 defines the essential information requirements for person-centred health and care records across the UK. This standard ensures that critical information about individuals receiving care is consistently captured, maintained and shared across different care settings and organisations.

The standard covers demographic details, clinical information, care preferences, legal status, and support needs - providing a comprehensive framework for holistic person-centred care delivery.

## Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder engagement with health and care professionals, patients and carers, this standard addresses the fundamental need for consistent, high-quality information sharing. It supports integrated care delivery by ensuring all professionals have access to the same core information about the people they support.

The standard aligns with UK health and care policy initiatives including integrated care systems, personalised care, and digital transformation programmes. It incorporates requirements from the Equality Act 2010, Mental Capacity Act 2005, and other relevant legislation.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Key Recommendations

The Core Information Standard supports various health and care interventions through comprehensive information capture and sharing:

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Person-centred care planning | Capture individual preferences, goals and support needs using 'About Me' and care planning sections | NICE NG86, NG97 | Strong |
| Safeguarding assessments | Document safeguarding concerns, looked after child status, and child protection plans systematically | Working Together 2023 | Mandatory |
| Reasonable adjustments | Record required adjustments for disabilities and impairments to ensure equitable access | Equality Act 2010 | Legal requirement |
| Mental capacity assessments | Document capacity assessments, best interests decisions, and lasting power of attorney details | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | Legal requirement |
| End of life care | Capture CPR decisions, advance statements, preferred place of death/care | NICE NG142, CG140 | Strong |
| Medication management | Maintain comprehensive medication records including changes, allergies and adverse reactions | NICE NG5 | Strong |
| Risk management | Document clinical risks, triggers, and contingency plans for proactive care | Various NICE guidelines | Moderate |

These recommendations ensure comprehensive capture of essential information to support safe, effective, person-centred care delivery across all settings.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Healthcare Professionals and Support Staff

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | General Practitioner (GP) | Primary care physician responsible for ongoing medical care, referrals and care coordination | Yes | Clinical assessment, care planning, medication management, mental capacity assessment |
| CISV2.P2 | Community Nurse | Delivers nursing care in community settings, supports care planning and coordinates with other services | Yes | Clinical procedures, medication administration, wound care, patient education |
| CISV2.P3 | Social Worker | Assesses social care needs, coordinates support services, manages safeguarding concerns | Yes | Needs assessment, safeguarding, mental capacity, care planning, resource coordination |
| CISV2.P4 | Hospital Clinician | Specialist doctors and consultants providing acute and specialist care | Yes | Diagnosis, treatment planning, investigations, discharge planning |
| CISV2.P5 | Allied Health Professional | Therapists providing specialised interventions (physio, OT, speech therapy etc.) | Yes | Functional assessment, therapy planning, equipment provision, rehabilitation |
| CISV2.P6 | Care Coordinator | Coordinates integrated care across multiple providers and settings | Yes | Care planning, service coordination, information sharing, review management |
| CISV2.P7 | Care Home Manager | Manages residential care settings and oversees resident care | Yes | Care planning, medication management, staff coordination, regulatory compliance |
| CISV2.P8 | Mental Health Professional | Specialists in mental health assessment and treatment | Yes | Mental health assessment, risk assessment, formulation, therapy provision |
| CISV2.P9 | Person/Patient/Service User | Individual receiving care who may input their own information | Yes | Self-advocacy, preference expression, goal setting, self-management |
| CISV2.P10 | Carer/Family Member | Informal carers and family members supporting the person | Yes | Daily care support, advocacy, information provision, care coordination |

**General Practitioner (GP)** - Primary care physicians use the standard to maintain comprehensive records, coordinate care across services, manage medications, and ensure continuity of care through transitions.

**Community Nurse** - These professionals rely on the standard to access care plans, document home visits, monitor chronic conditions, and coordinate with GPs and specialists.

**Social Worker** - Social workers utilise the standard to document assessments, manage safeguarding concerns, coordinate support packages, and ensure legal requirements are met.

**Hospital Clinician** - Specialists access historical information, document investigations and procedures, plan discharges, and communicate with primary care teams.

**Allied Health Professional** - Therapists use the standard to access functional assessments, document interventions, track progress, and coordinate equipment provision.

**Care Coordinator** - These key professionals use the full breadth of the standard to coordinate complex care across multiple providers and ensure information flows effectively.

**Care Home Manager** - Managers access resident preferences, care plans, risk assessments and legal information to ensure appropriate care delivery in residential settings.

**Mental Health Professional** - These specialists document mental health assessments, risk factors, formulations and treatment plans while accessing broader health and social context.

**Person/Patient/Service User** - Individuals can contribute their preferences, goals and 'About Me' information to ensure care is truly person-centred.

**Carer/Family Member** - Informal carers access care plans and contribute valuable information about the person's needs, preferences and daily routines.

title: Business Processes
content:
## Business Processes

The Core Information Standard supports key business processes across health and social care settings. These processes ensure consistent information capture, sharing and use throughout a person's care journey.

### Process Overview

The following processes represent critical workflows where the Core Information Standard enables effective information management:

- **Initial Registration and Assessment** - Capturing foundational person information and initial care needs
- **Care Planning and Review** - Developing and maintaining person-centered care plans
- **Clinical Encounter Documentation** - Recording consultations, assessments and interventions
- **Medication Management** - Safe prescribing, review and reconciliation processes
- **Referral and Transition** - Managing care transfers between services and settings
- **Safeguarding Response** - Identifying and managing safeguarding concerns
- **Legal Status Management** - Maintaining current legal information and capacity assessments
- **End of Life Planning** - Documenting preferences and anticipatory care decisions

processes_data.summary_markdown: Eight core business processes define how the Core Information Standard supports care delivery workflows. Each process maps key activities, information requirements and professional responsibilities to ensure comprehensive, person-centered documentation.

activities.title: Initial Registration and Assessment
activities.personas: General Practitioner (GP), Community Nurse, Social Worker
activities.objectives: Establish comprehensive baseline information including demographics, preferences, and initial care needs.

activities.title: Care Planning and Review
activities.personas: Care Coordinator, Social Worker, Community Nurse, Person/Patient/Service User
activities.objectives: Develop comprehensive person-centered care plans aligned with individual goals and preferences.

activities.title: Clinical Encounter Documentation
activities.personas: General Practitioner (GP), Hospital Clinician, Allied Health Professional, Mental Health Professional
activities.objectives: Comprehensively document clinical encounters including assessments, findings, and treatment decisions.

activities.title: Medication Management
activities.personas: General Practitioner (GP), Hospital Clinician, Community Nurse
activities.objectives: Ensure safe medication prescribing, monitoring, and reconciliation across care settings.

activities.title: Referral and Transition
activities.personas: General Practitioner (GP), Hospital Clinician, Care Coordinator, Social Worker
activities.objectives: Manage safe and effective transitions between care providers and settings.

activities.title: Safeguarding Response
activities.personas: Social Worker, General Practitioner (GP), Community Nurse, Care Home Manager
activities.objectives: Identify, assess and respond to safeguarding concerns for vulnerable individuals.

activities.title: Legal Status Management
activities.personas: Mental Health Professional, Social Worker, Hospital Clinician, Care Home Manager
activities.objectives: Maintain accurate legal status information including capacity, consent, and statutory powers.

activities.title: End of Life Planning
activities.personas: General Practitioner (GP), Hospital Clinician, Community Nurse, Person/Patient/Service User, Carer/Family Member
activities.objectives: Document and coordinate end of life preferences and anticipatory care decisions.

title: User Scenarios
content:
## Emergency Hospital Admission with Complex Needs

A 78-year-old person with dementia, diabetes, and limited mobility is brought to A&E following a fall at home. The paramedics need immediate access to critical information to ensure safe and appropriate care delivery.

**Personas involved:** Hospital Clinician, Community Nurse, Social Worker, Carer/Family Member

**Linked processes:** CISV2.C, CISV2.D, CISV2.E

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE15 | NHS number | Patient identification and record retrieval |
| CISV2.DE126 | Cognition | Understanding cognitive status and communication needs |
| CISV2.DE149 | Mobility | Assessing fall risk and mobility support requirements |
| CISV2.DE197 | Alerts | Identifying critical safety information |
| CISV2.DE639 | Problem list | Reviewing active medical conditions |
| CISV2.DE1264 | Allergies and adverse reactions | Ensuring medication safety |
| CISV2.DE1313 | Medications and medical devices | Current medication review for interactions |
| CISV2.DE285 | Lasting power of attorney for health and welfare or court-appointed deputy (or equivalent) | Identifying decision-makers if capacity lacking |

## Integrated Care Planning for Mental Health Crisis

A person experiencing mental health difficulties requires coordinated support across health, social care, and housing services following discharge from an acute mental health unit.

**Personas involved:** Mental Health Professional, Care Coordinator, Social Worker, General Practitioner (GP), Person/Patient/Service User

**Linked processes:** CISV2.B, CISV2.E, CISV2.G

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE52 | About me | Understanding person's perspective and preferences |
| CISV2.DE257 | Mental capacity assessment | Determining decision-making capacity |
| CISV2.DE326 | Mental health act or equivalent status | Legal framework for care delivery |
| CISV2.DE1212 | Formulation | Clinical understanding of mental health needs |
| CISV2.DE1238 | Risks | Risk assessment and management planning |
| CISV2.DE1432 | Care and support plan | Coordinated multi-agency care planning |
| CISV2.DE1506 | Contingency/safety plans | Crisis response protocols |
| CISV2.DE1537 | Advance statement | Person's preferences for future care |

## Child Protection Concerns in Primary Care

During a routine appointment, a GP identifies potential safeguarding concerns for a 6-year-old child. The situation requires immediate multi-agency coordination and information sharing.

**Personas involved:** General Practitioner (GP), Social Worker, Community Nurse

**Linked processes:** CISV2.A, CISV2.F

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE234 | Consent relating to child | Recording parental consent and child's views |
| CISV2.DE378 | Safeguarding | Documenting safeguarding concerns and actions |
| CISV2.DE407 | Looked after child | Checking care status |
| CISV2.DE422 | Child protection plan | Identifying existing protection measures |
| CISV2.DE452 | Professional contacts | Coordinating with involved professionals |
| CISV2.DE465 | Personal contacts | Understanding family dynamics |
| CISV2.DE1034 | Family history | Contextual family information |

## Complex Discharge Planning from Hospital

An elderly person with multiple conditions requires discharge from hospital to a care home setting, necessitating comprehensive handover of medical, social, and care needs.

**Personas involved:** Hospital Clinician, Care Home Manager, Social Worker, Allied Health Professional, Community Nurse

**Linked processes:** CISV2.C, CISV2.D, CISV2.E

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE70 | Individual requirements | Identifying specific support needs |
| CISV2.DE556 | Discharge details | Documenting discharge arrangements |
| CISV2.DE639 | Problem list | Current medical conditions requiring ongoing management |
| CISV2.DE1001 | Services and care | Care package arrangements |
| CISV2.DE1028 | Primary support reason | Categorizing main support needs |
| CISV2.DE1313 | Medications and medical devices | Medication transfer and reconciliation |
| CISV2.DE1398 | Equipment and adaptations | Necessary equipment for care home |
| CISV2.DE1405 | Plan and requested actions | Post-discharge care requirements |

## End of Life Care Planning at Home

A person with terminal cancer wishes to remain at home for end-of-life care. Their GP coordinates with community services to ensure appropriate support and symptom management.

**Personas involved:** General Practitioner (GP), Community Nurse, Carer/Family Member, Person/Patient/Service User

**Linked processes:** CISV2.B, CISV2.H

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE353 | Advance decision to refuse treatment (ADRT) | Documenting treatment preferences |
| CISV2.DE1615 | End of life care | Comprehensive end-of-life planning |
| CISV2.DE1616 | Cardio-pulmonary resuscitation (CPR) decision | Recording resuscitation decisions |
| CISV2.DE1643 | Estimated prognosis | Clinical prognosis documentation |
| CISV2.DE1689 | Priorities of care | Person's care priorities |
| CISV2.DE1713 | Anticipatory medicines/equipment | Symptom management preparations |
| CISV2.DE1736 | Preferred place of death | Recording person's wishes |
| CISV2.DE1764 | Preferred place of care | Ongoing care location preferences |

## Transition from Children's to Adult Services

A young person with complex disabilities is transitioning from children's to adult services at age 18, requiring comprehensive information transfer and care continuity.

**Personas involved:** Social Worker, Care Coordinator, Allied Health Professional, General Practitioner (GP), Carer/Family Member

**Linked processes:** CISV2.A, CISV2.B, CISV2.E

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE70 | Individual requirements | Documenting ongoing support needs |
| CISV2.DE99 | Impairment | Detailed disability information |
| CISV2.DE759 | Educational history | Educational support requirements |
| CISV2.DE1180 | Assessments | Comprehensive needs assessments |
| CISV2.DE1432 | Care and support plan | Transition planning documentation |
| CISV2.DE1398 | Equipment and adaptations | Equipment needs for adult services |
| CISV2.DE1584 | Additional support plans | Education and health care plans |

## Medication Review in Primary Care

A GP conducts a comprehensive medication review for a person with multiple long-term conditions who is experiencing side effects from their current medication regime.

**Personas involved:** General Practitioner (GP), Person/Patient/Service User

**Linked processes:** CISV2.C, CISV2.D

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE639 | Problem list | Reviewing conditions requiring treatment |
| CISV2.DE1064 | Investigation results | Recent test results affecting prescribing |
| CISV2.DE1264 | Allergies and adverse reactions | Checking for drug sensitivities |
| CISV2.DE1313 | Medications and medical devices | Current medication reconciliation |
| CISV2.DE1373 | Medication change summary cluster | Documenting medication changes |
| CISV2.DE1390 | Medication discontinued entry | Recording stopped medications |
| CISV2.DE859 | Drug/substance use | Considering interactions with substances |

scenarios_metadata.title: Emergency Hospital Admission with Complex Needs
Integrated Care Planning for Mental Health Crisis
Child Protection Concerns in Primary Care
End of Life Care Planning at Home
Transition from Children's to Adult Services

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Clinical Decision Tables

The Core Information Standard incorporates several key decision support mechanisms to guide safe and effective care delivery.

### Mental Capacity Assessment Decision Logic

**Title:** Mental Capacity Assessment Requirements
**Description:** Determines when formal mental capacity assessment is required and guides documentation requirements based on decision type and person's presentation.

| Condition | Required Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Person lacks capacity for specific decision AND no valid LPA/deputy in place | CISV2.DE257 (Mental capacity assessment), CISV2.DE262 (Assessment details), CISV2.DE263 (Document location) | Trigger best interests decision process, notify care team |
| Valid LPA for health and welfare exists | CISV2.DE285 (LPA details), CISV2.DE293 (LPA name), CISV2.DE294 (Contact details) | Route decisions to attorney, document consultation |
| Person under Mental Health Act detention | CISV2.DE326 (MHA status), CISV2.DE333 (Section type) | Apply statutory framework, consider capacity for non-treatment decisions |
| Urgent treatment required AND capacity unclear | CISV2.DE257 (Assessment), CISV2.DE1616 (CPR decision if applicable) | Proceed under best interests, document rationale, assess capacity when feasible |

### Safeguarding Alert Decision Logic

**Title:** Safeguarding Concern Escalation
**Description:** Guides professionals in identifying and escalating safeguarding concerns based on risk indicators and legal thresholds.

| Condition | Required Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Child with unexplained injuries OR concerning presentation | CISV2.DE378 (Safeguarding concerns), CISV2.DE380 (Indicator flag), CISV2.DE422 (Child protection plan status) | Immediate safeguarding referral, document concerns, check existing plans |
| Adult at risk AND unable to protect themselves | CISV2.DE378 (Safeguarding), CISV2.DE257 (Capacity assessment), CISV2.DE1238 (Risks) | Initiate adult safeguarding process, assess capacity for safety decisions |
| Existing safeguarding plan AND new concerns | CISV2.DE380 (Active indicator), CISV2.DE406 (Previous concerns) | Escalate to safeguarding lead, update risk assessment, convene review |
| Professional disagreement on threshold | CISV2.DE378 (Concerns), CISV2.DE452 (Professional contacts) | Seek senior consultation, document rationale, consider multi-agency discussion |

### Medication Safety Decision Logic

**Title:** High-Risk Medication Management
**Description:** Supports safe prescribing and monitoring for medications requiring special precautions.

| Condition | Required Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| New medication AND existing allergies | CISV2.DE1264 (Allergies), CISV2.DE1267 (Causative agent), CISV2.DE1313 (Current medications) | Cross-check allergen database, consider alternatives, document if proceeding |
| Polypharmacy (≥5 medications) AND age ≥65 | CISV2.DE1313 (Medication list), CISV2.DE10 (Date of birth), CISV2.DE639 (Problem list) | Trigger medication review, assess for interactions, consider deprescribing |
| Renal/hepatic impairment AND renally/hepatically cleared drugs | CISV2.DE639 (Problem list), CISV2.DE1064 (Investigation results - eGFR/LFTs), CISV2.DE1313 (Medications) | Calculate adjusted doses, increase monitoring frequency, document rationale |
| High-risk medication (anticoagulant, insulin, etc.) | CISV2.DE1313 (Medication item), CISV2.DE1352 (Dose directions), CISV2.DE1064 (Monitoring results) | Mandate counselling completion, schedule monitoring, provide safety information |

### Care Transition Decision Logic

**Title:** Safe Discharge Readiness Assessment
**Description:** Ensures all necessary elements are in place for safe transition between care settings.

| Condition | Required Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Discharge to home AND complex needs | CISV2.DE70 (Individual requirements), CISV2.DE1001 (Services and care), CISV2.DE1432 (Care plan) | Verify support package active, confirm equipment delivered, schedule follow-up |
| Discharge to care home | CISV2.DE556 (Discharge details), CISV2.DE1313 (Medications), CISV2.DE1398 (Equipment) | Generate transfer summary, reconcile medications, communicate special requirements |
| Cognitive impairment AND living alone | CISV2.DE126 (Cognition), CISV2.DE708 (Household composition), CISV2.DE1506 (Contingency plans) | Assess capacity for self-care, implement assistive technology, establish check-in protocol |
| End of life care transition | CISV2.DE1615 (EoL care preferences), CISV2.DE1736 (Preferred place of death), CISV2.DE1713 (Anticipatory medicines) | Confirm anticipatory medications available, verify DNACPR status, align with preferences |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Quality and Performance Indicators

These indicators support monitoring of care quality, safety, and person-centered outcomes across the Core Information Standard implementation.

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.01 | Completion of 'About Me' Information | Count of records with completed CISV2.DE52 (About me) sections including at least 3 subsections | Total active person records | By care setting type, age band, primary support reason (CISV2.DE1028) | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.02 | Mental Capacity Assessment Documentation | Count of records with documented capacity assessments (CISV2.DE257) where person has cognitive impairment (CISV2.DE126) | Total records with cognitive impairment documented | By setting, legal status (CISV2.DE326), presence of LPA (CISV2.DE285) | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.03 | Medication Review Timeliness | Count of records with medication review (CISV2.DE1364) within recommended timeframe based on risk factors | Total records with active medications (CISV2.DE1313) requiring review | By age group, polypharmacy status, high-risk medication flag | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.04 | Safeguarding Response Time | Count of safeguarding concerns (CISV2.DE378) with documented initial response within 24 hours | Total new safeguarding concerns raised | By concern type, person age group, urgency level | Weekly |
| CISV2.IND.05 | Care Plan Currency | Count of care plans (CISV2.DE1432) reviewed within planned review date (CISV2.DE1481) | Total active care plans with review dates set | By care coordinator (CISV2.DE6), complexity level, setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.06 | Allergy Alert Completeness | Count of records with allergy status documented (CISV2.DE1264) including 'no known allergies' confirmation | Total active person records | By care setting, recent admission status, age group | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.07 | Advance Care Planning Coverage | Count of records with documented advance decisions (CISV2.DE353) OR advance statements (CISV2.DE1537) for eligible persons | Total persons aged 18+ with life-limiting conditions (CISV2.DE639) | By diagnosis group, care setting, capacity status | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.08 | Professional Contact Documentation | Count of professional contacts (CISV2.DE506) with complete documentation including outcome (CISV2.DE526) | Total professional contacts recorded | By professional type (CISV2.DE514), contact type (CISV2.DE519), urgency | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.09 | Reasonable Adjustment Implementation | Count of identified reasonable adjustments (CISV2.DE72) with evidence of implementation in care delivery | Total reasonable adjustments identified | By adjustment type (CISV2.DE77), impairment category (CISV2.DE104), setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.10 | Risk Assessment Completeness | Count of high-risk persons (CISV2.DE1238) with documented mitigation plans (CISV2.DE1506) | Total persons with identified high risks | By risk type (CISV2.DE1243), care setting, review status | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.11 | Transition Planning Quality | Count of transitions (CISV2.DE476) with documented handover including all required elements | Total care transitions between services/settings | By transition type, complexity, receiving service readiness | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.12 | End of Life Preference Recording | Count of persons on EoL pathway with documented preferences for place of care (CISV2.DE1764) and death (CISV2.DE1736) | Total persons identified as end of life (CISV2.DE1643) | By care setting, prognosis awareness (CISV2.DE1666), support network | Monthly |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## System Requirements

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR.01 | Person Identification and Matching | System must support person identification using NHS number (CISV2.DE15) as primary identifier with ability to match using demographic details when NHS number unavailable | CISV2.A (Initial Registration), CISV2.E (Referral and Transition) | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.02 | Consent Management | Capture and enforce information sharing consents (CISV2.DE210) with granular control over data access based on purpose and recipient | All processes | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.03 | Professional Authentication | Authenticate professionals and maintain audit trail of access and modifications with professional details (CISV2.DE82-89 pattern) | All processes | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.04 | Care Plan Versioning | Maintain version history of care plans (CISV2.DE1432) with ability to track changes, review dates, and responsible professionals | CISV2.B (Care Planning and Review) | High |
| CISV2.FR.05 | Alert Generation and Display | Generate and prominently display clinical alerts (CISV2.DE197) based on allergies, safeguarding concerns, and risk assessments | CISV2.C (Clinical Encounter), CISV2.F (Safeguarding Response) | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.06 | Medication Reconciliation | Support medication reconciliation across care transitions with ability to compare medication lists and document changes (CISV2.DE1373) | CISV2.D (Medication Management), CISV2.E (Referral and Transition) | High |
| CISV2.FR.07 | Multi-Agency Access Control | Enable controlled access for professionals from different organizations based on role and legitimate relationship | CISV2.B (Care Planning), CISV2.F (Safeguarding Response) | High |
| CISV2.FR.08 | Structured Assessment Recording | Capture structured assessments (CISV2.DE1180) with validated scoring mechanisms and decision support integration | CISV2.A (Initial Assessment), CISV2.B (Care Planning) | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.09 | Legal Status Tracking | Track and alert on time-bound legal statuses (CISV2.DE326 MHA status, CISV2.DE312 DoLS) with expiry notifications | CISV2.G (Legal Status Management) | High |
| CISV2.FR.10 | Document Management | Store and retrieve clinical documents (CISV2.DE1787) with appropriate metadata, versioning, and access controls | All processes | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.11 | Workflow Orchestration | Support clinical workflows with task assignment, escalation paths, and deadline monitoring | CISV2.B (Care Planning), CISV2.E (Referral and Transition) | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.12 | Emergency Access Override | Provide break-glass functionality for emergency access with full audit trail and post-access review | CISV2.C (Clinical Encounter) | High |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR.01 | Response Time - Critical Data | Critical clinical data (allergies, medications, alerts) must be retrievable within 2 seconds 99% of the time | Performance | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.02 | Availability | System must maintain 99.9% availability during core hours (08:00-20:00) with degraded service options for critical functions | Availability | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.03 | Data Encryption | All data must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+) with key management compliant with NHS standards | Security | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.04 | Audit Logging | Comprehensive audit logging of all access and modifications with tamper-proof storage for 20 years | Security/Compliance | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.05 | Concurrent User Support | Support minimum 1000 concurrent users per deployment with linear scalability | Scalability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.06 | Mobile Responsiveness | Full functionality available on mobile devices with offline capability for critical functions | Usability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.07 | Accessibility Compliance | Meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards with screen reader compatibility and keyboard navigation | Accessibility | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.08 | Disaster Recovery | Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour | Reliability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.09 | Interoperability Standards | Support HL7 FHIR R4 UK Core for all clinical data exchange with backwards compatibility for v3 messaging | Interoperability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.10 | Clinical Terminology Support | Native support for SNOMED CT, dm+d, LOINC with real-time terminology service integration | Interoperability | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.11 | Performance Degradation | System must maintain core functionality with degraded performance when 50% over expected load | Performance | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR.12 | Data Retention Compliance | Configurable retention policies compliant with NHS Records Management Code of Practice | Compliance | High |
